votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the logic of the Coq specification, handling the cases for n <= 0, n == 1, and n > 1 exactly as defined.

The Python code correctly implements the piecewise logic of the Coq specification for all concrete inputs.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec: it returns False when n <= 0 (matching n > 0 requirement), returns result == 1 when n == 1, and returns result == 18 * 10^(n-2) when n > 1. The disjunction structure in Coq (n=1 case OR n>1 case) is handled by the if-else in Python, producing the same concrete results.
